bids.reasoning: This is the opening bid at t=0, so I should start with my maximum utility offer. All issues have the same utility structure where valueE gives 1.0 utility, so my ideal bid is valueE for every issue. This gives me a utility of 1.0 * (0.1 + 0.1 + 0.2 + 0.3 + 0.3) = 1.0. Starting high leaves room to concede later.

The opponent opened with all valueA (minimum utility for me: 0.2 across all issues), which is their best offer for themselves. It's very early (t=0.02), so I should stay near my ideal bid. I'll maintain high values on my most important issues (issueD and issueE, weight 0.3 each) and make a tiny concession on lower-weight issues to signal willingness to negotiate without giving much away.

The opponent is holding very firm on their preferred values (all A's), with only a tiny concession on issueE (from valueA to valueB). We're very early in the negotiation (t=0.04), so I should stay close to my top utility while making small concessions. My highest-weight issues are issueD and issueE (0.3 each), so I'll hold firm there. I can concede slightly on issueA and issueB (low weight 0.1 each) and make a small move on issueC. I'll offer a bid slightly below my last one to show movement without giving away too much.

The opponent is barely conceding - they've moved from all valueA to only slight changes (issueE: valueB once, issueC: valueB once), and they seem to be holding firm near their best values. We're still very early (t=0.06), so I don't need to concede much yet. My highest-weight issues are issueD and issueE (0.3 each), so I should hold firm there. I can concede a bit more on issueA and issueB (weight 0.1 each) since they matter less to me. I'll try offering valueB on issueA and issueB while keeping issueC at valueD and issueD/issueE at valueE to maintain high utility (~0.84).

The opponent is holding very firm on issueA, issueB at valueA, and mostly valueA for issueD and issueE, with slight movement on issueC (valueB) and issueD (valueC in one offer). My highest-weight issues are issueD and issueE (0.3 each), so I should hold firm there. The opponent seems to care most about getting low values on issueA, issueB, and issueC. Since issueA and issueB have low weight for me (0.1 each), I can concede those to valueA to signal goodwill, while protecting issueD and issueE. I'll try offering valueA on issueA and issueB (low importance to me), valueC on issueC (moderate concession), and keep issueD and issueE at valueE to protect my high-weight issues. This gives me utility: 0.1*0.2 + 0.1*0.2 + 0.2*0.6 + 0.3*1.0 + 0.3*1.0 = 0.02+0.02+0.12+0.30+0.30 = 0.76.

The opponent seems to be holding firm on issueA and issueB at valueA, while slowly conceding on issueD (now at valueB) and issueE (now at valueD). Since it's still early (t=0.10), I should hold firm on my high-weight issues (issueD and issueE) while conceding more on low-weight issues. I'll keep issueD and issueE at valueE, concede issueC to valueC, and give issueA and issueB to valueA (already at minimum weight for me).
